en:
2. Automatic Writing. —A second automatic phenomenon, which at the outset corresponds to a higher degree of partial hypnosis, is automatic writing. It is, according to my experience, much rarer and more difficult to produce than table-turning. As in table-turning, it is again a matter of a primary suggestion, to the conscious when sensibility is retained, to the unconscious when it is obliterated. The suggestion is, however, not a simple one, for it already bears in itself an intellectual element. "To write" means "to write something." This special element of the suggestion, which extends beyond the merely motor, often conditions a certain perplexity on the part of the subject, giving rise to slight contrary suggestions which hinder the appearance of the automatisms. I have observed in a few cases that the suggestion is realised, despite its relative venturesomeness ( e.g. one directed towards the waking consciousness of a so-called normal person). However, it takes place in a peculiar way; it first displaces the purely motor part of the central system concerned in hypnosis, and the deeper hypnosis is then reached by auto-suggestion from the motor phenomenon, analogous to the procedure in table-turning described above. The subject, [37] who has a pencil in his hand, is purposely engaged in conversation whilst his attention is diverted from the writing. The hand begins to make movements, beginning with many upward strokes and zigzag lines, or a simple line is made. Occasionally it happens that the pencil does not touch the paper, but writes in the air. These movements must be conceived as purely motor phenomena, which correspond to the expression of the motor element in the presentation "write." This phenomenon is somewhat rare; generally single letters are first written, and what was said above of table-turning holds true of their combination into words and sentences. True mirror-writing is also observed here and there. In the majority of cases, and perhaps in all experiments with beginners who are not under some very special suggestion, the automatic writing is that of the subject. Occasionally its character may be greatly changed, [38] but this is secondary, and is always to be regarded as a symptom of the intruding synthesis of a subconscious personality.

Fig. 3.

As stated, the patient's automatic writing never came to any very great development. In these experiments, generally carried out in darkness, she passed over into semi-somnambulism, or into ecstasy. The automatic writing had thus the same effect as the preliminary table-turning.
zh:
2. **自動書寫**——第二種自動現象，即自動書寫，起初對應於較高程度的局部催眠。根據我的經驗，它比桌靈轉動更為罕見且更難產生。如同桌靈轉動，這同樣涉及一個主要暗示：當意識保留時，它作用於意識；當意識被抹除時，則作用於無意識。然而，這個暗示並非簡單的，因為它本身已包含一個智性元素。「書寫」意味著「書寫某些東西」。這個超越純粹運動層面的特殊暗示元素，常常在受試者方面引發某種困惑，產生輕微的相反暗示，從而阻礙自動現象的出現。我在少數案例中觀察到，儘管暗示相對大膽（例如，針對所謂正常人的清醒意識），它仍得以實現。然而，它以一種奇特的方式發生：它首先置換了催眠所涉及的中樞系統中純粹運動的部分，然後透過運動現象的自我暗示達到更深的催眠，類似於上述桌靈轉動的程序。受試者[37]手握鉛筆，刻意參與對話，同時其注意力從書寫上被轉移。手開始做出動作，起初是許多向上劃的筆畫和鋸齒線，或畫出一條簡單的線條。偶爾會發生鉛筆沒有接觸紙張，而是在空中書寫的情況。這些動作必須被理解為純粹的運動現象，對應於「書寫」這個表象中運動元素的表達。這種現象較為罕見；通常首先寫出單個字母，而上述關於桌靈轉動的論述，同樣適用於這些字母組合成單詞和句子。真正的鏡像書寫也時有觀察到。在大多數情況下，或許在所有對初學者進行的實驗中（除非他們處於某種非常特殊的暗示之下），自動書寫都是受試者本人的書寫。偶爾，其特徵可能發生巨大變化，[38]但這是次要的，且始終應被視為潛意識人格侵入性綜合的症狀。

圖 3。

如前所述，患者的自動書寫從未發展到非常高的程度。在這些通常於黑暗中進行的實驗中，她過渡到半夢遊狀態或出神狀態。因此，自動書寫產生了與初步桌靈轉動相同的效果。
